title: Leandra urophylla
intro: Leandra urophylla, also known as "erva-prieto," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The health benefits of Leandra urophylla include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leandra urophylla is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Leandra urophylla is classified within the family Melastomataceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family.
